content:
First, observe that “\(n^2\) has the same remainder mod 7, mod 11 and mod 13” means there is an integer \(r\) with
\[
n^2\equiv r\pmod7,\quad
n^2\equiv r\pmod{11},\quad
n^2\equiv r\pmod{13}.
\]
By CRT this is equivalent to
\[
n^2\equiv r\pmod{1001},\qquad 0\le r\le 6
\]
(since \(r\) must lie below 7 to match the mod 7–residue).

We break \(1\le n\le2017\) into two full blocks of length 1001 and a small remainder:

• In each complete block of length 1001 there are
\[
A=\sum_{n_7\in\Bbb Z/7}\;\bigl(\#\{x\bmod11:x^2\equiv n_7^2\}\bigr)\,\bigl(\#\{y\bmod13:y^2\equiv n_7^2\}\bigr)
\]
solutions.  One finds \(A=17\).  
Thus the first 1001 integers contribute 17, the next 1001 also 17, totalling \(2\cdot17=34\).

• Finally we must check the tail \(n=2003,2004,\dots,2017\).  Equivalently these are the residues \(n\equiv1,2,\dots,15\pmod{1001}\).  A quick check of \(n=1,2,\dots,15\) shows only 
\[
n=1\quad(\,n^2\equiv1,1,1\,),\quad
n=2\quad(\,n^2\equiv4,4,4\,)
\]
work.  Hence there are 2 more in the last 15.

Putting it all together yields
\[
2\cdot17 \;+\;2 \;=\;34+2\;=\;36.
\]
Therefore the number of positive integers \(n<2018\) with \(n^2\) having the same remainder mod 7, 11, 13 is 
\[
\boxed{36}.
\]

message.content:
First, observe that “\(n^2\) has the same remainder mod 7, mod 11 and mod 13” means there is an integer \(r\) with
\[
n^2\equiv r\pmod7,\quad
n^2\equiv r\pmod{11},\quad
n^2\equiv r\pmod{13}.
\]
By CRT this is equivalent to
\[
n^2\equiv r\pmod{1001},\qquad 0\le r\le 6
\]
(since \(r\) must lie below 7 to match the mod 7–residue).

We break \(1\le n\le2017\) into two full blocks of length 1001 and a small remainder:

• In each complete block of length 1001 there are
\[
A=\sum_{n_7\in\Bbb Z/7}\;\bigl(\#\{x\bmod11:x^2\equiv n_7^2\}\bigr)\,\bigl(\#\{y\bmod13:y^2\equiv n_7^2\}\bigr)
\]
solutions.  One finds \(A=17\).  
Thus the first 1001 integers contribute 17, the next 1001 also 17, totalling \(2\cdot17=34\).

• Finally we must check the tail \(n=2003,2004,\dots,2017\).  Equivalently these are the residues \(n\equiv1,2,\dots,15\pmod{1001}\).  A quick check of \(n=1,2,\dots,15\) shows only 
\[
n=1\quad(\,n^2\equiv1,1,1\,),\quad
n=2\quad(\,n^2\equiv4,4,4\,)
\]
work.  Hence there are 2 more in the last 15.

Putting it all together yields
\[
2\cdot17 \;+\;2 \;=\;34+2\;=\;36.
\]
Therefore the number of positive integers \(n<2018\) with \(n^2\) having the same remainder mod 7, 11, 13 is 
\[
\boxed{36}.
\]
